task_completion.description: OpenToonz application is launched and a new scene is initialized.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz main interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Scene or Camera resolution is set to 2048 x 858.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Camera Settings' or 'Scene Settings' window, the Width is set to 2048 and the Height is set to 858.

task_completion.description: Frame rate is set to 24 fps and frame range is set to 1 to 120.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Scene Settings' or 'Output Settings' window, the Frame Rate (fps) is 24, and the Start/End frames (or From/To) are set to 1 and 120.

task_completion.description: The scene is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Scene' dialog shows the path '/home/ga/OpenToonz/projects/cinema_short/' and the filename 'cinema_short.tnz', or the OpenToonz window title bar displays this file path.

task_completion.description: Output settings are configured to render a PNG to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Output Settings' window, the 'Save In' path is set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/cinema_scope_test/' and the file format is set to 'png'.
A render progress bar, a completed render window, or a file manager showing the newly created PNG file in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/cinema_scope_test/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to configure the scene rather than command-line tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenToonz interface, settings dialogs, and menus being actively used.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create or edit the .tnz XML file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) are shown editing 'cinema_short.tnz'.

integrity.description: The render output was generated by OpenToonz, not mocked via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz render window or output settings are visible, and there is no terminal window showing commands like 'touch' or 'cp' to fake the PNG file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The scene must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/projects/cinema_short/cinema_short.tnz'. The resolution must be exactly 2048 x 858 pixels. The frame rate must be 24 fps. The frame range must be 1 to 120. The render output must be a PNG saved to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/cinema_scope_test/'.
